svg.title: How the job tracking agent works
svg.desc: The flow starts with the user applying to jobs manually. Employers and job boards then send responses over time to Gmail: confirmations, assessments, interview invites, and rejections. The automated agent then fetches emails read-only, classifies each with a local Llama 3.2 model returning type, company, role and confidence, routes funnel events to the tracker while skipping listings and noise, and appends a deduplicated row to the Google Sheets tracker which builds up the application history over time.
svg.text: Automated agent, runs on my own machine

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does the read-only Gmail fetch work?')
sendPrompt('What does the local classifier return for each email?')

g.text: keep funnel events, skip the rest

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does the duplicate check and append work?')
